rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-dichlorination of aromatic compounds and alpha,beta-unsaturated substrates using Cl3CCN as the chlorine source via paired electrolysis
electrochemical_role: Paired electrolysis: cathodic reduction of Cl3CCN releases a chloride anion and a neutral radical A, which is further reduced to dichloroacetonitrile anion B; at the anode, chloride (from cathode and/or from TEAC supporting electrolyte) is oxidized to molecular Cl2, which then chlorinates the substrate

key_intermediates.name: molecular chlorine (Cl2) generated at the anode
chloronium-like / electrophilic chlorination intermediate on aromatic (1 -> 2)

electron_flow: At the cathode, Cl3CCN accepts an electron to release Cl- and radical A, which gains a second electron to form the dichloroacetonitrile anion B; simultaneously, at the anode, chloride (from B-derived release and from TEAC) is oxidized to Cl2, which then electrophilically chlorinates the arene or alpha,beta-unsaturated substrate to give the 1,2-dichloride product.

probe_reagents.name: N-allyl-N-(2-propenyl)-p-toluenesulfonamide probe 5 (chloride vs chloride-radical probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Tetrachlorinated open-chain product 6 isolated in 76% yield (consistent with Cl2/Cl+); the cyclized pyrrolidine product 7 (diagnostic of Cl radical) was not detected, supporting chlorine (Cl2) rather than Cl* as the active species

probe_reagents.name: KI-starch test (atmosphere on 1 mol scale of 1b)
probe_reagents.observation: Positive KI-starch response confirmed the presence of Cl2 in the reaction

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of reaction mixtures (acetyl aniline 1b and cinnamide 3a with Et4NCl / Cl3CCN)
probe_reagents.observation: Both mixtures share peaks at ca. -1.7 V vs SCE (reduction of Cl3CCN) and +1.8 V vs SCE (oxidation of chloride), and scans starting from either polarity give identical patterns, indicating a single dominant pathway involving cathodic reduction of Cl3CCN and anodic oxidation of Cl-

notes: Authors explicitly distinguish chlorine (Cl2) from chlorine radical using the diallylsulfonamide probe 5: trapping a Cl* would have given cyclized pyrrolidine 7, but only the open tetrachloride 6 formed, arguing against a radical-chain Cl* pathway. For alpha,beta-unsaturated substrates, 1 equiv TEAC is required because no proton is generated to recycle Cl-; the Et4N+ pairs with anion B. The chlorination step itself is described as independent of the electrochemical process (electrophilic addition of in situ Cl2), so the role of electrolysis is on-demand Cl2 generation from Cl3CCN.
